codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly enhances GET /health with node sync status via check_node_async. The code adds a 'synced' field (true/false/null) with 5s timeout protection, preserves existing 503 behavior for chain.height failures, and follows project conventions (lazy imports, error logging, type annotations). Duck-typing via type(e).__name__ == 'NodeNotSynced' is a pragmatic choice given brownie's lazy import architecture. 7 comprehensive unit tests cover all expected behaviors. All validators passed (pytest 117, mypy clean, ruff clean).

issues.description: test_timeout_protection uses asyncio.sleep(10) which means the test waits the full 5s timeout before passing. Consider using a shorter timeout in tests or patching the timeout value to speed up the test suite.

Duck-typing check `type(e).__name__ == 'NodeNotSynced'` is fragile if ypricemagic renames the exception class. However, this is a reasonable trade-off given the lazy import constraints, and is unlikely to break without a major ypricemagic version bump.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The health endpoint now has up to 5s additional latency from check_node_async timeout. This latency behavior isn't documented in the library knowledge base. Workers or operators debugging slow health checks should know about this timeout.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.py:112 — asyncio.wait_for(check_node_async(), timeout=5.0). Not mentioned in .factory/library/architecture.md or user-testing.md.

sharedStateObservations.observation: user-testing.md lists health endpoint as a test surface but doesn't document the new 'synced' field or expected values (true/false/null). Future workers testing health endpoints should know what to expect.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/user-testing.md lists 'GET /health' but doesn't mention synced field. The actual response now includes {status, chain, block, synced}.

summary: PASS. The enhanced health endpoint implementation is clean and complete. It correctly adds node sync status checking with check_node_async, implements proper timeout protection (5s), handles all exception paths (synced=True on success, synced=False on NodeNotSynced, synced=None on timeout/other errors), and preserves existing 503 behavior when chain.height fails. 7 unit tests comprehensively cover all expected behaviors from the feature spec. All four validation contract items (VAL-CORE-021 through VAL-CORE-024) are addressed. Two non-blocking issues noted: slow timeout test and fragile duck-typing for exception matching.
